codeReview.summary: The implementation correctly satisfies all feature requirements. The worker captured a live full-page screenshot of the running app (`docs/readme-quote-latest-block.png`, ~119KB) showing a USDC→crvUSD quote on Ethereum block 24582349 with 47s age, added 4 lines to README.md under a new '### Quote UI screenshot (latest block)' subsection inside '## Browser UI', and ran the full validator suite (pytest 298 passed, ruff lint/format, mypy). The markdown image embed uses proper syntax at line 267 with descriptive alt text. Both VAL-DOCS-006 and VAL-DOCS-015 are fully satisfied.

sharedStateObservations.observation: During agent-browser usage the worker encountered an error 'Browser not launched. Call launch first' when trying to chain multiple browser commands in a single Execute call after the `open` command had already been issued in a prior call. The worker recovered by re-issuing `open` before the subsequent commands. This is a subtle agent-browser behavior: each Execute call is a new shell, so chaining `open` and then other browser commands in separate Execute calls requires re-issuing `open` if the session state is stale. The frontend-worker SKILL.md does not document this recovery pattern or the fact that `open` may need to be re-called after a failed chained command.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows: `agent-browser --session "37d9c45b50f0" snapshot -i` returned 'Browser not launched. Call launch first.' after a prior successful `open` call. Worker recovered by re-running `open http://localhost:8000` before subsequent commands.

summary: Clean pass. 2-file change (README.md + docs/readme-quote-latest-block.png) fully satisfies all expectedBehavior items and both VAL-DOCS-006/VAL-DOCS-015 assertions. Screenshot clearly shows USDC→crvUSD quote result, block 24582349, 47s age, and from→to conversion. Image placement and alt text are appropriate. All automated validators passed. One minor shared-state observation: agent-browser recovery behavior around re-issuing 'open' after stale session state is not documented in the frontend-worker skill.
